skill.name: subagent-orchestration
skill.description: Guides users through coordinating multiple subagents, defining task boundaries, and deciding when parallel agent work is appropriate.
skill.summary: Coordinate independent subagent tasks with clear scopes and constraints.
skill.author: DieGopherLT

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick execution patterns. Review found these are false positives from Markdown front matter, ordinary prose, file-scope guidance, and a Markdown code fence; no executable code, network behavior, data access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern In Prose
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings match ordinary documentation text and JSON field names, such as description content and workflow wording. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, encryption API, or security-sensitive implementation is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain Markdown or JSON prose only. There is no code path that uses DES, MD5, SHA1, RC4, or another weak cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Interpreted As Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command finding points to a Markdown fenced template block. It is documentation for scoping subagent tasks, not Ruby backtick execution or shell command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact location is a Markdown code fence containing placeholder task fields. No executable script, interpreter directive, or command invocation exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: File Scope Guidance Is Not Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings refer to guidance about files, directories, and documentation references for bounded subagent tasks. The skill does not enumerate local files, run discovery commands, or collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a documentation list of workflow anti-patterns and file-boundary advice. No command, API call, or instruction to probe the host environment is present.

content.value_statement: Parallel subagent work can create overlap, missed context, and conflicting edits. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code define independent tasks with clear file boundaries.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when multiple subagent tasks can run in parallel.
Provides a scope template with task, file, constraint, and reference sections.
Encourages explicit files to modify and files not to touch.
Separates independent tasks from sequential tasks that depend on earlier results.
Prompts users to include reference documents for subagent context.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not launch subagents by itself.
It does not enforce file boundaries after agents begin work.
It requires the user to provide accurate task scope and context.
It is not a project management system or scheduling engine.

use_cases.title: Split Independent Code Reviews
use_cases.description: Coordinate separate review agents for unrelated modules while keeping each review scope clear.

use_cases.title: Delegate Parallel Implementation Tasks
use_cases.description: Assign independent implementation work to different agents without overlapping file ownership.

use_cases.title: Design Safer Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Create repeatable delegation patterns that include boundaries, constraints, and reference documents.

prompt_templates.title: Check Agent Fit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request to decide whether subagents are useful.
prompt_templates.prompt: Should I coordinate subagents for this task? Review the work, identify independent parts, and tell me whether parallel agents are appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Draft One Subagent Scope
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a bounded task for one delegated agent.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a subagent task scope for this work. Include the task, files to modify, files not to touch, constraints, and reference documents.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Parallel Agents
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan parallel subagent work for these independent tasks. Give each agent a clear scope, excluded files, constraints, and required context.

prompt_templates.title: Audit A Delegation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this multi-agent plan for overlap, missing exclusions, hidden dependencies, and unclear context. Rewrite it into safe parallel scopes.

output_examples.input: Coordinate agents to review authentication and billing changes.
output: Recommended approach: use parallel agents only if the modules are independent.
Authentication agent reviews authentication flow, tests, and related documentation.
Billing agent reviews billing flow, tests, and related documentation.
Each agent receives explicit files not to touch.

output_examples.input: Launch multiple agents for a refactor and test update.
output: The refactor and test update may be sequential if tests depend on refactor results.
Run analysis first, then delegate implementation and test updates after boundaries are clear.
Add reference documents before assigning each subagent.

output_examples.input: Review this delegation plan before agents start.
output: The plan needs clearer excluded files for each agent.
One task depends on another task result, so it should not run in parallel.
Add business constraints and exact reference documents for each scope.

best_practices: Define exact files to modify and exact files not to touch for every subagent.
Use parallel agents only when tasks are independent and non-overlapping.
Provide reference documents and constraints before delegation begins.
anti_patterns: Delegating vague tasks such as update related files.
Running dependent tasks in parallel before earlier results are known.
Omitting exclusions that prevent agents from changing unrelated files.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps structure parallel subagent work with clear tasks, file boundaries, constraints, and context.

faq.question: Does it run agents automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides coordination guidance and templates. The user or host tool controls agent execution.

faq.question: When should I avoid parallel agents?
faq.answer: Avoid them when tasks share files, depend on earlier results, or cannot be scoped clearly.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The concepts apply to Codex, Claude, and Claude Code workflows that support delegated agent work.

faq.question: How does it reduce conflicts?
faq.answer: It requires explicit ownership, excluded files, and constraints before agents begin work.

faq.question: Is this a security tool?
faq.answer: No. It is a workflow guidance skill for safer coordination, not a scanner or enforcement system.
